name: Loanmax Title Loans
description_short: LoanMax Title Loans offers auto title loans up to $10,000 across multiple states. Founded 1990, requires only your vehicle, photo ID, and clear title.
description_long:
LoanMax Title Loans was founded in 1990 in Jonesboro, Georgia, making it one of the longest-operating title loan companies in the United States. With over 35 years in business, the company describes itself as helping 'hardworking Americans use the equity in their vehicle to access the cash they need.' It operates across multiple states including Nevada, Virginia, Delaware, South Carolina, New Mexico, and Michigan, with a corporate complaint address in Alpharetta, GA. In Nevada, it runs at least five Las Vegas-area locations.

LoanMax offers cash loans secured by a vehicle title, with amounts up to $10,000 subject to vehicle evaluation and the borrower's ability to repay. The application process requires just three things: the vehicle itself, a government-issued photo ID, and a clear (lien-free) vehicle title. Accepted repayment methods include cash, check, debit card, and Western Union. Customers can also pay online through the company's portal or call the national toll-free line at 877-511-CASH.

What distinguishes LoanMax is its multi-decade operating history and its multi-state licensed footprint, which comes with state-specific regulatory disclosures that suggest a degree of compliance infrastructure uncommon in the title loan space. The company is licensed in Virginia under License CFI-246, licensed in Delaware by the State Bank Commissioner, and regulated in New Mexico by the Financial Institutions Division. This level of state-by-state licensing transparency is relatively unusual for a title lender. The website also offers a Spanish-language option, indicating some effort to serve bilingual borrowers.

Honestly assessed, LoanMax is a high-cost lender of last resort. No APR or interest rate information appears anywhere on the website — a significant transparency gap that makes it impossible to evaluate the true cost of borrowing before visiting a branch. Nevada law itself requires LoanMax to post the disclaimer that 'title loans should be used for short-term financial needs only and not as a long-term financial solution,' and that borrowers with credit difficulties should seek credit counseling before proceeding. Loan approval and amount are subject to vehicle appraisal, meaning the $10,000 maximum is not guaranteed. Borrowers risk losing their vehicle if they default.
pros: Founded in 1990 — over 35 years of operating history
Five Las Vegas-area locations, reducing travel burden for Nevada borrowers
Loans up to $10,000 — higher ceiling than most payday or cash advance products
Minimal documentation required: vehicle, photo ID, and clear title only
Accepts multiple repayment methods including Western Union and debit card
Licensed and regulated in six states with state-specific consumer disclosures posted publicly
Spanish-language option available on the website
cons: No APR, interest rate, or fee information disclosed anywhere on the website
Nevada regulators mandate a warning that title loans are not a long-term financial solution
Loan amount is not guaranteed — subject to vehicle appraisal at the branch
Borrowers risk vehicle repossession if they default
Limited hours (closes 6PM weekdays, 2PM Saturdays) with no 24/7 or online-only option
best_for: Vehicle owners who need emergency cash quickly and cannot qualify for a personal loan or credit card
Borrowers who need more than a typical payday loan ($1,000–$10,000 range) and own their car outright
Nevada residents who prefer in-person lending with multiple nearby branch options
Consumers who have already exhausted lower-cost alternatives and understand the collateral risk
diagnosis: LoanMax Title Loans is best suited for vehicle owners facing a short-term cash emergency who have no access to lower-cost credit and own their car free and clear. The main caveat is that the company discloses no rates online whatsoever, and the Nevada regulatory warning embedded in their own site signals these are expensive, high-risk products — borrowers who default can lose their vehicle.
services: Auto title loans (car title loans) up to $10,000
In-store loan origination at multiple Las Vegas locations
Phone loan inquiries via national line 877-511-CASH
State-specific consumer disclosures and licensing information
